advantages: pathlib gives readable, object-oriented, cross-platform path handling.
Convenience methods (read_text, write_text, glob, mkdir) reduce boilerplate.
os.environ keeps secrets and config out of source code.
Standard library — no extra dependency to install.
disadvantages: Two overlapping APIs (os.path vs pathlib) can confuse newcomers.
read_text/write_text load whole files into memory — not ideal for very large files.
Some third-party libraries still expect string paths, requiring str(path) conversions.
os-level operations can be platform-specific in edge cases (permissions, symlinks).
